The **2007–08 season** was the 128th season of competitive [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") in England.

## Club football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club football")]

### European competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: European competitions")]

See also: [2007–08 UEFA Champions League](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_UEFA_Champions_League "2007–08 UEFA Champions League"), [2007–08 UEFA Cup](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_UEFA_Cup "2007–08 UEFA Cup"), and [2007 UEFA Intertoto Cup](/wiki/2007_UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "2007 UEFA Intertoto Cup")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/05/Poolvschels.jpg/250px-Poolvschels.jpg)](/wiki/File:Poolvschels.jpg)

[Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") and [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool_F.C. "Liverpool F.C.") faced each other in the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") for the fourth season in succession.

In October 2007, [Arsenal](/wiki/Arsenal_F.C. "Arsenal F.C.") equalled the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") record victory with a 7–0 win over [Slavia Prague](/wiki/SK_Slavia_Prague "SK Slavia Prague") at the [Emirates Stadium](/wiki/Emirates_Stadium "Emirates Stadium").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The record was broken the following month when [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool_F.C. "Liverpool F.C.") defeated [Beşiktaş](/wiki/Be%C5%9Fikta%C5%9F_J.K. "Beşiktaş J.K.") 8–0 at [Anfield](/wiki/Anfield "Anfield").[[2]](#cite_note-2) All four English clubs competing in the [Champions League](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_UEFA_Champions_League "2007–08 UEFA Champions League") reached the quarter-finals, resulting in three all-English ties during the competition's latter stages. Liverpool eliminated Arsenal in the quarter-finals, but lost the semi-final to [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C."), who went on to meet [Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United_F.C. "Manchester United F.C.") in the [final](/wiki/2008_UEFA_Champions_League_Final "2008 UEFA Champions League Final") in Moscow. United completed the European [Double](/wiki/Double_(association_football) "Double (association football)"), winning the Premier League two points ahead of Chelsea and winning the [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League"), again against Chelsea 6–5 on penalties (1–1 after extra time) to lift the European Cup for the third time. This was a unique occurrence – the first time two English clubs had met in the final of the European Cup/Champions League. It was also a repeat of the opening game of the season, the [FA Community Shield](/wiki/FA_Community_Shield "FA Community Shield"), which also finished in a 1–1 draw and saw a United win 3–0 on penalties.

In the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_UEFA_Cup "2007–08 UEFA Cup"), none of the English teams taking part reached the quarter-final stage. [Blackburn Rovers](/wiki/Blackburn_Rovers_F.C. "Blackburn Rovers F.C."), who had qualified for the competition via the [Intertoto Cup](/wiki/2007_UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "2007 UEFA Intertoto Cup"), were beaten in the first round by [AEL](/wiki/Athlitiki_Enosi_Larissa_F.C. "Athlitiki Enosi Larissa F.C."). The three other English clubs progressed through the group stages, with [Bolton Wanderers](/wiki/Bolton_Wanderers_F.C. "Bolton Wanderers F.C.") losing to [Sporting CP](/wiki/Sporting_Clube_de_Portugal "Sporting Clube de Portugal"), while [Tottenham Hotspur](/wiki/Tottenham_Hotspur_F.C. "Tottenham Hotspur F.C.") and [Everton](/wiki/Everton_F.C. "Everton F.C.") were both eliminated on penalty shootouts in the round of 16, by [PSV Eindhoven](/wiki/PSV_Eindhoven "PSV Eindhoven") and [Fiorentina](/wiki/ACF_Fiorentina "ACF Fiorentina") respectively. The [2008 UEFA Cup final](/wiki/2008_UEFA_Cup_final "2008 UEFA Cup final") was held at the [City of Manchester Stadium](/wiki/City_of_Manchester_Stadium "City of Manchester Stadium"), the first time that the [UEFA Cup Final](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_Final "UEFA Cup Final") had been held in England since being reduced to a single match. The event was marred by [riots in Manchester city centre](/wiki/2008_UEFA_Cup_final_riots "2008 UEFA Cup final riots") prior to the game.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In the match itself, [Zenit Saint Petersburg](/wiki/FC_Zenit_Saint_Petersburg "FC Zenit Saint Petersburg") beat [Rangers](/wiki/Rangers_F.C. "Rangers F.C.") 2–0 to lift the trophy.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### Premier League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Premier League")]

Main article: [2007–08 Premier League](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Premier_League "2007–08 Premier League")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3d/C_ronaldo_cropped.jpg/250px-C_ronaldo_cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:C_ronaldo_cropped.jpg)

[Cristiano Ronaldo](/wiki/Cristiano_Ronaldo "Cristiano Ronaldo") was the Premier League's top scorer and won numerous awards.

[Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United_F.C. "Manchester United F.C.") retained the [Premier League](/wiki/Premier_League "Premier League") title, winning the competition for the tenth time and becoming [champions of England](/wiki/English_football_champions "English football champions") for the seventeenth time. The title was decided on the final day of the season as United's 2–0 win at [Wigan Athletic](/wiki/Wigan_Athletic_F.C. "Wigan Athletic F.C.") saw them crowned champions and consigned [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") to the runners-up spot regardless of their result at home to [Bolton Wanderers](/wiki/Bolton_Wanderers_F.C. "Bolton Wanderers F.C."). [Arsenal](/wiki/Arsenal_F.C. "Arsenal F.C.") and [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool_F.C. "Liverpool F.C.") qualified for the [UEFA Champions League 2008–09 third qualifying round](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_UEFA_Champions_League#Third_qualifying_round "2008–09 UEFA Champions League") by finishing third and fourth respectively, while [Everton](/wiki/Everton_F.C. "Everton F.C.")'s fifth position gave them a place in the [2008–09 UEFA Cup first round](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_UEFA_Cup#First_round "2008–09 UEFA Cup"). [Reading](/wiki/Reading_F.C. "Reading F.C."), [Birmingham City](/wiki/Birmingham_City_F.C. "Birmingham City F.C.") and [Derby County](/wiki/Derby_County_F.C. "Derby County F.C.") were relegated. Derby became the first team in Premier League history to be relegated before the end of March.[[5]](#cite_note-5) They eventually finished on the lowest Premier League points tally ever, amassing only eleven points and winning just one game all season.

The Premier League underwent a major rebranding; changing its sponsored name from the [Barclays](/wiki/Barclays_plc "Barclays plc") Premiership to the Barclays Premier League, introducing a revamped logo and new typeface for players' jerseys.

### Football League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Football League")]

Main article: [2007–08 Football League](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Football_League "2007–08 Football League")

#### Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Championship")]

After the disappointment of a play-off final defeat the previous year, [West Bromwich Albion](/wiki/West_Bromwich_Albion_F.C. "West Bromwich Albion F.C.") won the Football League Championship title and returned to the Premier League. [Stoke City](/wiki/Stoke_City_F.C. "Stoke City F.C.") secured the other automatic promotion spot, ending a 23-year absence from the top flight. [Hull City](/wiki/Hull_City_A.F.C. "Hull City A.F.C.") followed them by winning the [play-off final](/wiki/2008_Football_League_Championship_playoff_final "2008 Football League Championship playoff final"), beating [Bristol City](/wiki/Bristol_City_F.C. "Bristol City F.C.") 1–0 at [Wembley Stadium](/wiki/Wembley_Stadium "Wembley Stadium") in the final to reach the top division of English football for the first time in their 104-year history.[[6]](#cite_note-6) It was the first time that Hull had played at either the original or rebuilt Wembley Stadium. Despite impressing on their Championship debut in 2006–07, [Colchester United](/wiki/Colchester_United_F.C. "Colchester United F.C.") finished bottom this season and were relegated back to League One. [Scunthorpe United](/wiki/Scunthorpe_United_F.C. "Scunthorpe United F.C.")'s first journey into the Championship since the 1960s proved short-lived, and they also went back down. The biggest story however was [Leicester City](/wiki/Leicester_City_F.C. "Leicester City F.C.")'s relegation, as a lack of stability at the club (with no fewer than eight men, including caretakers, occupying the manager's seat over the season) proved their undoing and sent them down to the third tier for the first time in their history.

#### League One

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: League One")]

In a season mired by controversy and points deductions at both ends of the table, [Swansea City](/wiki/Swansea_City_A.F.C. "Swansea City A.F.C.") were the clear champions in League One. In terms of results, [Leeds United](/wiki/Leeds_United_A.F.C. "Leeds United A.F.C.") were actually the best team behind Swansea, but had started the season on –15 points following their failure to reach an agreement with [HM Revenue & Customs](/wiki/HM_Revenue_%26_Customs "HM Revenue & Customs") on their Creditors Voluntary Arrangement. This was the first time in the league's history that such a penalty had been imposed. [Nottingham Forest](/wiki/Nottingham_Forest_F.C. "Nottingham Forest F.C.") therefore took the second automatic promotion spot on the final day of the season after a late surge of form, culminating in Forest defeating [Yeovil](/wiki/Yeovil_Town_F.C. "Yeovil Town F.C.") 3–2 (Who ironically defeated them in the play-off semi-final the previous season). [Doncaster Rovers](/wiki/Doncaster_Rovers_F.C. "Doncaster Rovers F.C.") won promotion to the Championship by beating Leeds 1–0 at Wembley Stadium in the League One play-off final, thus returning to the top two tiers for the first time since 1958. At the opposite end of the table, Port Vale were in fact the worst team going by results, but [Luton Town](/wiki/Luton_Town_F.C. "Luton Town F.C.") went into administration and were deducted ten points causing them to finish bottom, though they would have been relegated even without this penalty. [Bournemouth](/wiki/AFC_Bournemouth "AFC Bournemouth") also received a ten-point deduction for going into administration, and in their case it did prove fatal, sending the club down to League Two. If the points deduction did not occur, then [Crewe](/wiki/Crewe_Alexandra_F.C. "Crewe Alexandra F.C.") would have gone down. [Gillingham](/wiki/Gillingham_F.C. "Gillingham F.C.") were the other team to suffer relegation.

#### League Two

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: League Two")]

[Milton Keynes Dons](/wiki/Milton_Keynes_Dons_F.C. "Milton Keynes Dons F.C.") won their first honours as a club, winning the [League Two](/wiki/Football_League_Two "Football League Two") title and the [Football League Trophy](/wiki/Football_League_Trophy "Football League Trophy"). The other clubs automatically promoted were runners-up [Peterborough United](/wiki/Peterborough_United_F.C. "Peterborough United F.C."), who had pushed the Dons close for most of the season, and [Hereford United](/wiki/Hereford_United_F.C. "Hereford United F.C."), who returned to the third level of English football for the first time in thirty years. [Stockport County](/wiki/Stockport_County_F.C. "Stockport County F.C.") won promotion to League One by beating [Rochdale](/wiki/Rochdale_A.F.C. "Rochdale A.F.C.") 3–2 at Wembley Stadium in the League Two play-off final. There was to be no repeat of [Wrexham](/wiki/Wrexham_A.F.C. "Wrexham A.F.C.")'s last day heroics of the 2006–07 season, and this time they finished bottom and went out of the League. Despite a good run late in the season, [Mansfield Town](/wiki/Mansfield_Town_F.C. "Mansfield Town F.C.") joined them. Both [Dagenham & Redbridge](/wiki/Dagenham_%26_Redbridge_F.C. "Dagenham & Redbridge F.C.") and [Morecambe](/wiki/Morecambe_F.C. "Morecambe F.C.") played in the [Football League](/wiki/English_Football_League "English Football League") for the first time, after securing promotion to League Two from the [Conference](/wiki/Conference_National "Conference National").

### Cup competitions and Community Shield

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Cup competitions and Community Shield")]

Main articles: [2007–08 FA Cup](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_FA_Cup "2007–08 FA Cup"), [2007–08 Football League Cup](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Football_League_Cup "2007–08 Football League Cup"), [2007–08 Football League Trophy](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Football_League_Trophy "2007–08 Football League Trophy"), and [2007 FA Community Shield](/wiki/2007_FA_Community_Shield "2007 FA Community Shield")

[Portsmouth](/wiki/Portsmouth_F.C. "Portsmouth F.C.") won the [FA Cup](/wiki/2008_FA_Cup_Final "2008 FA Cup Final") with a 1–0 victory over [Cardiff City](/wiki/Cardiff_City_F.C. "Cardiff City F.C."). [Tottenham Hotspur](/wiki/Tottenham_Hotspur_F.C. "Tottenham Hotspur F.C.") won the [League Cup](/wiki/2008_Football_League_Cup_Final "2008 Football League Cup Final"), beating the holders [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") 2–1 after extra time. It was the first League Cup final to be played at the new [Wembley Stadium](/wiki/Wembley_Stadium "Wembley Stadium").[[7]](#cite_note-7) [MK Dons](/wiki/Milton_Keynes_Dons_F.C. "Milton Keynes Dons F.C.") won the [Football League Trophy](/wiki/Football_League_Trophy_2007%E2%80%9308 "Football League Trophy 2007–08") after beating [Grimsby Town](/wiki/Grimsby_Town_F.C. "Grimsby Town F.C.") 2–0 in the final.[[8]](#cite_note-8) [Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United_F.C. "Manchester United F.C.") took the first silverware of the season when they beat [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") 3–0 on penalties in the [FA Community Shield](/wiki/2007_FA_Community_Shield "2007 FA Community Shield").

### Non-League football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Non-League football")]

Main articles: [Football Conference 2007–08](/wiki/Football_Conference_2007%E2%80%9308 "Football Conference 2007–08"), [Conference League Cup 2007–08](/wiki/Conference_League_Cup_2007%E2%80%9308 "Conference League Cup 2007–08"), and [FA Trophy 2007–08](/wiki/FA_Trophy_2007%E2%80%9308 "FA Trophy 2007–08")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/Exeter-Blue-Square-Premier-08-Celebrate-crop.jpg/250px-Exeter-Blue-Square-Premier-08-Celebrate-crop.jpg)](/wiki/File:Exeter-Blue-Square-Premier-08-Celebrate-crop.jpg)

[Exeter City](/wiki/Exeter_City_F.C. "Exeter City F.C.") celebrate their 2008 Conference National play-off final win.

The [Conference National](/wiki/Conference_National "Conference National") became known as the [Blue Square](/wiki/The_Rank_Group#CITEREF2013 "The Rank Group") Premier upon the announcement of a three-year sponsorship deal. The two regional feeder leagues became known as the Blue Square North and South respectively.[[9]](#cite_note-9) The deal signalled the end of a nine-year association between the Conference and the [Nationwide Building Society](/wiki/Nationwide_Building_Society "Nationwide Building Society"). [Aldershot Town](/wiki/Aldershot_Town_F.C. "Aldershot Town F.C.") won the Conference National to gain automatic promotion to the Football League, while [Exeter City](/wiki/Exeter_City_F.C. "Exeter City F.C.") beat [Cambridge United](/wiki/Cambridge_United_F.C. "Cambridge United F.C.") 1–0 in the [play-off final](/wiki/Conference_National_Playoff_Final_2008 "Conference National Playoff Final 2008") at [Wembley Stadium](/wiki/Wembley_Stadium "Wembley Stadium") to secure the other promotion place. Aldershot also won the [Conference League Cup](/wiki/Conference_League_Cup "Conference League Cup"), beating [Rushden & Diamonds](/wiki/Rushden_%26_Diamonds_F.C. "Rushden & Diamonds F.C.") in a [penalty shootout](/wiki/Penalty_shootout_(association_football) "Penalty shootout (association football)") after a 3–3 draw. The [FA Trophy](/wiki/FA_Trophy "FA Trophy") was won by [Ebbsfleet United](/wiki/Ebbsfleet_United_F.C. "Ebbsfleet United F.C."), who beat [Torquay United](/wiki/Torquay_United_F.C. "Torquay United F.C.") 1–0 at Wembley.

## Events off the field

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Events off the field")]

[Deloitte](/wiki/Deloitte "Deloitte") reported in September 2007 that transfer spending by all Premier League and Football League clubs had exceeded £500 million, compared to £300 million spent the season before. Deloitte attributed the rise in spending to the huge increase in broadcasting revenue and new owners buying into Premier League clubs.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Two league clubs moved to new grounds for 2007–08. [Shrewsbury Town](/wiki/Shrewsbury_Town_F.C. "Shrewsbury Town F.C.") moved to the [New Meadow](/wiki/New_Meadow "New Meadow") for the start of this season, after leaving their old stadium, [Gay Meadow](/wiki/Gay_Meadow "Gay Meadow"). [Milton Keynes Dons](/wiki/Milton_Keynes_Dons_F.C. "Milton Keynes Dons F.C.") moved to their new 22,000 all seater stadium at [Denbigh, Milton Keynes](/wiki/Denbigh,_Milton_Keynes "Denbigh, Milton Keynes"), known as [Stadium:mk](/wiki/Stadium:mk "Stadium:mk"). The stadium was officially opened on 29 November 2007 by [Queen Elizabeth](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

[Sheffield](/wiki/Sheffield_F.C. "Sheffield F.C."), the world's oldest football club, celebrated their 150th anniversary; events included a friendly match against [Inter Milan](/wiki/Inter_Milan "Inter Milan").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Diary of the season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Diary of the season")]

1 July 2007: [Tottenham Hotspur](/wiki/Tottenham_Hotspur_F.C. "Tottenham Hotspur F.C.") pay a club record £16.5 million for [Charlton Athletic](/wiki/Charlton_Athletic_F.C. "Charlton Athletic F.C.") striker [Darren Bent](/wiki/Darren_Bent "Darren Bent").

2 July 2007: Manchester United shell out a combined total of £30 million for Brazilian midfielder [Anderson](/wiki/Anderson_(footballer,_born_1988) "Anderson (footballer, born 1988)") (from [Porto](/wiki/FC_Porto "FC Porto")) and [Portuguese](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal") winger [Nani](/wiki/Nani_(footballer) "Nani (footballer)") (from [Sporting CP](/wiki/Sporting_Clube_de_Portugal "Sporting Clube de Portugal")).

4 July 2007: [Everton](/wiki/Everton_F.C. "Everton F.C.") pay £4 million for [Sheffield United](/wiki/Sheffield_United_F.C. "Sheffield United F.C.") defender [Phil Jagielka](/wiki/Phil_Jagielka "Phil Jagielka").

5 July 2007: Aston Villa sign West Ham United midfielder [Nigel Reo-Coker](/wiki/Nigel_Reo-Coker "Nigel Reo-Coker") for £8.5 million.

10 July 2007: West Ham United pay a club record £7.5 million for Liverpool striker [Craig Bellamy](/wiki/Craig_Bellamy "Craig Bellamy"), while Wigan Athletic break their own record fee with a £5.3 million deal for West Bromwich Albion midfielder [Jason Koumas](/wiki/Jason_Koumas "Jason Koumas").

13 July 2007: Liverpool pay £11.5 million to [Ajax](/wiki/AFC_Ajax "AFC Ajax") for winger [Ryan Babel](/wiki/Ryan_Babel "Ryan Babel").

23 July 2007: [Freddie Ljungberg](/wiki/Freddie_Ljungberg "Freddie Ljungberg"), Arsenal's longest-serving player, leaves after nearly nine years in a £3 million move to West Ham United.

24 July 2007: [Robbie Fowler](/wiki/Robbie_Fowler "Robbie Fowler") leaves Liverpool for a second time to join Cardiff City on a free transfer.

3 August 2007: Manchester United sell striker [Alan Smith](/wiki/Alan_Smith_(footballer,_born_1980) "Alan Smith (footballer, born 1980)") to Newcastle United for £6 million.

4 August 2007: Leeds United, who have spent the summer on the brink of expulsion from the Football League due to their financial problems, are told that they will be allowed to take their place in League One, but will start on –15 points for exiting administration without a CVA. Leeds chairman [Ken Bates](/wiki/Ken_Bates "Ken Bates") immediately announces his intention to appeal against the penalty.

5 August 2007: Manchester United beat [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") on penalties after a 1–1 draw to seal the [Community Shield](/wiki/2007_FA_Community_Shield "2007 FA Community Shield") at [Wembley Stadium](/wiki/Wembley_Stadium "Wembley Stadium").

15 August 2007: Long-serving Aston Villa defender [Mark Delaney](/wiki/Mark_Delaney_(footballer) "Mark Delaney (footballer)") retires from playing at the age of 31 after failing to fully recover from a succession of injuries.

25 August 2007: [Ray Jones](/wiki/Ray_Jones_(footballer,_born_1988) "Ray Jones (footballer, born 1988)"), 18-year-old [Queens Park Rangers](/wiki/Queen%27s_Park_Rangers_F.C. "Queen's Park Rangers F.C.") striker, dies in a car crash in east London which claims the lives of two other people.

28 August 2007: Manchester United's long-serving [Norwegian](/wiki/Norway "Norway") striker [Ole Gunnar Solskjær](/wiki/Ole_Gunnar_Solskj%C3%A6r "Ole Gunnar Solskjær") retires from playing at the age of 34.

29 August 2007: [Martin Allen](/wiki/Martin_Allen "Martin Allen"), who was appointed manager of [Leicester City](/wiki/Leicester_City_F.C. "Leicester City F.C.") only three months ago, is dismissed from his job. Everton pay a club record £11.25 million to Middlesbrough for striker [Yakubu](/wiki/Yakubu_(footballer) "Yakubu (footballer)").

31 August 2007: August ends with Chelsea top after four matches and looking to reclaim their title from Manchester United, who currently stand 10th after a slow start. Manchester City and Wigan Athletic are surprise members of the current top four, along with Liverpool. Everton, Arsenal and Newcastle United complete the top seven while Bolton Wanderers, Fulham and Derby County make up the relegation zone. In the Championship, Coventry City and Ipswich Town are joint leaders of the division while Wolverhampton Wanderers, Stoke City, recently relegated Watford and Colchester United stand in the play-offs. The table is propped up by QPR, Preston North End and Sheffield Wednesday.

11 September 2007: [Ian Porterfield](/wiki/Ian_Porterfield "Ian Porterfield"), who scored the winning goal for [Sunderland](/wiki/Sunderland_A.F.C. "Sunderland A.F.C.") in the [1973 FA Cup Final](/wiki/1973_FA_Cup_Final "1973 FA Cup Final"), dies of cancer aged 61. Porterfield was managing [Armenia](/wiki/Armenia_national_football_team "Armenia national football team") at the time of his death, and earlier in his management career he had been in charge of teams including Sheffield United and Chelsea.

13 September 2007: [Gary Megson](/wiki/Gary_Megson "Gary Megson"), former West Bromwich Albion and [Nottingham Forest](/wiki/Nottingham_Forest_F.C. "Nottingham Forest F.C.") manager, ends his 18-month exile from football to take over at Leicester City.

20 September 2007: [José Mourinho](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Mourinho "José Mourinho"), who in the last three years has guided Chelsea to two [Premier League](/wiki/Premier_League "Premier League") titles, two [League Cups](/wiki/Football_League_Cup "Football League Cup") and an [FA Cup](/wiki/FA_Cup "FA Cup") triumph, resigns as manager after falling out with owner [Roman Abramovich](/wiki/Roman_Abramovich "Roman Abramovich"). Director of Football [Avram Grant](/wiki/Avram_Grant "Avram Grant") takes over control of the first team, becoming the first [Israeli](/wiki/Israel "Israel") manager in English football.

30 September 2007: At the end of September Arsenal have leaped to the top of the Premiership, followed closely by Manchester United. Manchester City are making a surprise title push and stand in third, while Liverpool, Everton, Portsmouth and Blackburn Rovers are also attempting to make their mark in the push for Europe. Tottenham Hotspur, Bolton and Derby are joint bottom of the Premiership, although Derby's vastly inferior goal difference makes them appear the most vulnerable of the three sides. In the Championship, Watford and Charlton Athletic, relegated from the Premiership last season, are pushing for an immediate return to the top flight and stand first and third respectively, sandwiching West Bromwich Albion. Newly promoted sides Bristol City (4th) and Scunthorpe United (6th) are pushing for a second consecutive promotion, while fifth-placed Stoke City are pushing for a return to the top tier after a 20-year absence. The same three teams from August stand in the bottom three, although now QPR has dropped below Preston and Sheffield Wednesday to stand bottom.

8 October 2007: [Peter Taylor](/wiki/Peter_Taylor_(footballer,_born_1953) "Peter Taylor (footballer, born 1953)") is sacked as manager of [Crystal Palace](/wiki/Crystal_Palace_F.C. "Crystal Palace F.C.") after just 16 months in charge.

11 October 2007: [Neil Warnock](/wiki/Neil_Warnock "Neil Warnock") returns to management with [Crystal Palace](/wiki/Crystal_Palace_F.C. "Crystal Palace F.C."), just five months after leaving Sheffield United.

17 October 2007: Bolton Wanderers, bottom of the Premier League, sack manager [Sammy Lee](/wiki/Sammy_Lee_(footballer) "Sammy Lee (footballer)") after six months in charge.

19 October 2007: [Steve Bruce](/wiki/Steve_Bruce "Steve Bruce") resigns after six years as manager of [Birmingham City](/wiki/Birmingham_City_F.C. "Birmingham City F.C."). Speculations mounts that he is about to take over from [Chris Hutchings](/wiki/Chris_Hutchings "Chris Hutchings") as manager of Wigan Athletic, where he had a brief spell in 2001.

25 October 2007: Gary Megson defects from Leicester City after six weeks as manager to take over at Bolton Wanderers, while [Martin Jol](/wiki/Martin_Jol "Martin Jol") is sacked as manager of Tottenham Hotspur (battling relegation from the Premier League after two successive fifth-place finishes) after three years.

31 October 2007: Arsenal are continuing their bid for a first title in four years and finish October joint top with Manchester United, but with a game in hand. Manchester City are also continuing their push for European qualification and stand in third, although they are facing pressure from Chelsea, Blackburn, Liverpool and Portsmouth. The relegation zone is unchanged from the end of September, although only three points separate 14th-placed Fulham from 20th-placed Derby. In the Championship, Watford stand six points clear of nearest challengers Bristol City and look well placed for a quick return to the Premiership, while West Brom, Ipswich, Wolves and Stoke (joint sixth with Plymouth Argyle) compete for the play-off places. Norwich City now prop up the Championship, and are three points adrift of the nearest two sides, QPR and Crystal Palace.

5 November 2007: [Chris Hutchings](/wiki/Chris_Hutchings "Chris Hutchings") is sacked as manager of Premier League strugglers Wigan Athletic after less than six months in charge.

21 November 2007: [England](/wiki/England_national_football_team "England national football team") fail to qualify for [Euro 2008](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_2008 "UEFA Euro 2008") after losing 3–2 to [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia_national_football_team "Croatia national football team") in their final qualifying game at [Wembley Stadium](/wiki/Wembley_Stadium "Wembley Stadium").

22 November 2007: [Steve McClaren](/wiki/Steve_McClaren "Steve McClaren") is dismissed as England manager by [The Football Association](/wiki/The_Football_Association "The Football Association") after 16 months in charge.

26 November 2007: [Billy Davies](/wiki/Billy_Davies "Billy Davies") is sacked as manager of [Derby County](/wiki/Derby_County_F.C. "Derby County F.C."), bottom of the Premier League with just one win so far.

28 November 2007: [Paul Jewell](/wiki/Paul_Jewell "Paul Jewell") returns to management with Derby County, six months after leaving Wigan Athletic. [Alex McLeish](/wiki/Alex_McLeish "Alex McLeish") resigns as [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland_national_football_team "Scotland national football team") manager to take over at Birmingham City.

30 November 2007: Arsenal finish November three points ahead of Manchester United with a game still in hand, although only seven points separate Arsenal from seventh-placed Portsmouth, and the teams in between – United, Manchester City, Chelsea, Liverpool and Aston Villa – all look capable of sustaining a title bid if they can string together a few good results. Derby remain bottom of the Premiership, but are now joined in the bottom three by Wigan and Sunderland. In the Championship, West Brom have cut Watford's lead to only two points. Charlton have recovered from a poor October to stand third, joined in the top six by Wolves, Ipswich and Bristol City. Norwich remain bottom, along with Preston, but are now only a point adrift of 21st-placed Blackpool, who lead QPR only by goal difference.

14 December 2007: [Fabio Capello](/wiki/Fabio_Capello "Fabio Capello"), 61-year-old Italian who has achieved great success with several top European clubs, accepts an offer from [The Football Association](/wiki/The_Football_Association "The Football Association") (FA) to take charge of the England team.

21 December 2007: Premier League strugglers Fulham sack manager [Lawrie Sanchez](/wiki/Lawrie_Sanchez "Lawrie Sanchez") after eight months in charge.

29 December 2007: [Phil O'Donnell](/wiki/Phil_O%27Donnell_(footballer) "Phil O'Donnell (footballer)"), former [Sheffield Wednesday](/wiki/Sheffield_Wednesday_F.C. "Sheffield Wednesday F.C.") midfielder, dies from a heart attack during a [Scottish Premier League](/wiki/Scottish_Premier_League "Scottish Premier League") game for [Motherwell](/wiki/Motherwell_F.C. "Motherwell F.C.").

30 December 2007: [Roy Hodgson](/wiki/Roy_Hodgson "Roy Hodgson") returns to English football, nine years after being sacked by [Blackburn Rovers](/wiki/Blackburn_Rovers_F.C. "Blackburn Rovers F.C."), to take over as Fulham manager.

31 December 2007: The year closes with Arsenal still top, although they have failed to take advantage of their game in hand and are only two points clear of Manchester United. It appears now that the title will end up either at Old Trafford or the Emirates – Chelsea, Liverpool, Manchester City, Everton and Aston Villa appear to be now competing just for European qualification. At the other end of the table, Derby are ten points adrift of safety and look certain for relegation. They are joined in the drop zone by Wigan and Fulham. A division below, West Brom have leapfrogged Watford into first. Bristol City are ramping up the pressure on their Hertfordshire rivals and are below them only by goal difference. Stoke, Charlton and Plymouth complete the top six. Norwich have escaped the relegation zone after a good run of results and their local rivals Colchester now prop up the table, along with Sheffield Wednesday and Preston.

7 January 2008: Fabio Capello officially takes over as England manager.

9 January 2008: [Sam Allardyce](/wiki/Sam_Allardyce "Sam Allardyce") is sacked after eight months in charge of [Newcastle United](/wiki/Newcastle_United_F.C. "Newcastle United F.C.").

11 January 2008: Chelsea pay Bolton Wanderers £15 million for French striker [Nicolas Anelka](/wiki/Nicolas_Anelka "Nicolas Anelka").

16 January 2008: [Kevin Keegan](/wiki/Kevin_Keegan "Kevin Keegan") is appointed manager of Newcastle United for the second time, having previously been their manager from 1992 until 1997.

24 January 2008: [George Burley](/wiki/George_Burley "George Burley") quits [Southampton](/wiki/Southampton_F.C. "Southampton F.C.") to take over as Scotland manager.

29 January 2008: [Dennis Wise](/wiki/Dennis_Wise "Dennis Wise") resigns as manager of [Leeds United](/wiki/Leeds_United_F.C. "Leeds United F.C.") (who replace him with former captain [Gary McAllister](/wiki/Gary_McAllister "Gary McAllister")) to become executive director of Newcastle United.

30 January 2008: Tottenham Hotspur pay £9 million for [Rangers](/wiki/Rangers_F.C. "Rangers F.C.") and Scotland defender [Alan Hutton](/wiki/Alan_Hutton "Alan Hutton").

31 January 2008: Manchester United lead Arsenal by goal difference as January closes, although Chelsea have undergone a renaissance under Avram Grant and are only four points off the Premiership summit. The West London club are ten points clear of fourth-placed Everton, who, along with Aston Villa, Manchester City and Liverpool, are now competing for the final Champions League place. The relegation zone is unchanged from the end of December. West Brom and Bristol City are joint top of the Championship, with Watford, Stoke, Charlton and Crystal Palace in the top six and Preston, Scunthorpe and Colchester in the bottom three.

6 February 2008: England beat [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland_national_football_team "Switzerland national football team") 2–1 in a home friendly in Fabio Capello's first game in charge.

24 February 2008: Tottenham Hotspur end their nine-year trophy drought with a 2–1 win over Chelsea in the Football League Cup final.

28 February 2008: Arsenal have regained their three-point advantage over Manchester United at the top of the Premiership, with Chelsea nine points behind their London rivals in third. Everton, Liverpool, Aston Villa and Portsmouth continue their European push. At the other end of the table, Derby are 14 points adrift of safety and appear effectively relegated. With eleven games left to play, Reading and Fulham join the Midlands club in the bottom three. In the Championship, Stoke have jumped to the top of the table and are fighting with Bristol City and Watford for the Football League title. West Brom, Plymouth and Charlton compete for the play-off places. The Championship relegation zone is unchanged.

1 March 2008: [Colchester United](/wiki/Colchester_United_F.C. "Colchester United F.C.")'s former [England](/wiki/England_national_football_team "England national football team") striker [Teddy Sheringham](/wiki/Teddy_Sheringham "Teddy Sheringham"), who turns 42 next month and is the oldest professional footballer in the country, announces that he will retire from playing at the end of the season, calling time on a 26-year playing career.

29 March 2008: Derby County's relegation is confirmed after they can only manage a 2–2 draw at home to fellow Premier League strugglers Fulham – the earliest relegation to be confirmed in all 16 seasons of the Premier League.

31 March 2008: Manchester United have established a five-point lead over Chelsea with six games left to play; Arsenal have suffered a slump in form and are in third. Liverpool have also established a five-point lead, but ahead of Everton in the race for the final Champions League place. Everton, Portsmouth and Blackburn now appear to be competing for UEFA Cup qualification. For Fulham and Bolton the challenge is to escape the drop zone, although with only six games left their hopes are becoming increasingly slimmer. Derby are already relegated and are playing now only to avoid breaking Sunderland's record, set two seasons ago, of the lowest points tally ever accrued in a Premiership season. In the Championship, only two points separate Bristol City, Stoke and a Hull City side making a late bid for promotion, although West Brom are only four points off the top with two games in hand. Watford and Wolves make up the rest of the top six. Colchester United appear doomed, being thirteen points behind 21st-placed Southampton with only fifteen left to play for. Scunthorpe United, while above Colchester, also seem destined for the drop. Sheffield Wednesday, however, are only a point behind Southampton and with two games in hand they still have a good chance of survival.

29 April 2008: Tottenham Hotspur match their club record fee with a £16.5 million move for [Dinamo Zagreb](/wiki/GNK_Dinamo_Zagreb "GNK Dinamo Zagreb") and Croatia midfielder [Luka Modrić](/wiki/Luka_Modri%C4%87 "Luka Modrić").

30 April 2008: With two matches left, Manchester United and Chelsea are level on 81 points; Arsenal are four points behind and look like they will be settling for third. Liverpool have sealed their fourth-place finish, while Everton seem destined for fifth despite late pressure from Aston Villa and Portsmouth. Derby have confirmed their 20th-place finish, but Fulham and Birmingham City are still hopeful of securing their top-flight survival at the expense of Reading, Bolton or Middlesbrough. The Championship has one round of matches left to play: West Brom's superior goal difference makes it likely that they will be automatically promoted, barring a 12-goal swing in the favour of third placed Hull. Second placed Stoke need to avoid defeat or hope that Hull loses on the final day to seal automatic promotion. Bristol City have sealed their place in the play-offs, while Watford, Crystal Palace, Wolves, Ipswich and Sheffield United fight for the final two play-off spots. Scunthorpe and Colchester are relegated, while Southampton are 22nd, but Leicester, Sheffield Wednesday, Coventry and Blackpool could all still be relegated.

11 May 2008: Manchester United seal their 10th Premier League title and their 17th top division title overall by beating Wigan Athletic at the [JJB Stadium](/wiki/DW_Stadium "DW Stadium") in Wigan, while Derby County finish the season in bottom place with a record top division low of one win and 11 points. Joining them in the Football League Championship for next season are Birmingham City and [Reading](/wiki/Reading_F.C. "Reading F.C.").

12 May 2008: [Porto](/wiki/FC_Porto "FC Porto") and [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal_national_football_team "Portugal national football team") defender [José Bosingwa](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Bosingwa "José Bosingwa") agrees to join Chelsea in a £16.2 million deal.

17 May 2008: Portsmouth's 58-year wait for a major trophy ends with a 1–0 win over Cardiff City in the [FA Cup](/wiki/FA_Cup "FA Cup") final. [Nwankwo Kanu](/wiki/Nwankwo_Kanu "Nwankwo Kanu") scores the only goal of the game.

21 May 2008: In the first all-English Champions League final, Manchester United beat Chelsea on penalties after a 1–1 draw in Moscow. [Edwin van der Sar](/wiki/Edwin_van_der_Sar "Edwin van der Sar") saves Nicolas Anelka's penalty kick to give the trophy to United, while earlier in the shoot-out [John Terry](/wiki/John_Terry "John Terry") misses the penalty that would have given Chelsea the trophy for the very first time.

24 May 2008: [Hull City](/wiki/Hull_City_A.F.C. "Hull City A.F.C.") reach the top flight for the first time in their history with a 1–0 win over [Bristol City](/wiki/Bristol_City_F.C. "Bristol City F.C.") in the [Football League Championship](/wiki/Football_League_Championship "Football League Championship") play-off final, with 39-year-old striker [Dean Windass](/wiki/Dean_Windass "Dean Windass") scoring the winning goal. It is Hull's third promotion in five seasons – the second-quickest rise from the league's fourth tier to the highest. On the same day, Avram Grant is sacked as manager of Chelsea (without a major trophy for the first time in four years) despite having taken them to the brink of Premier League and Champions League glory this season.

25 May 2008: [Doncaster Rovers](/wiki/Doncaster_Rovers_F.C. "Doncaster Rovers F.C.") reach the second tier of the English league for the first time in 50 years with a 1–0 win over [Leeds United](/wiki/Leeds_United_F.C. "Leeds United F.C.") in the [League One](/wiki/Football_League_One "Football League One") playoff final. Just six years ago, Leeds were a top-five [Premier League](/wiki/Premier_League "Premier League") club playing European football, while Doncaster were still in the [Conference](/wiki/Football_Conference "Football Conference").

2 June 2008: [Sven-Göran Eriksson](/wiki/Sven-G%C3%B6ran_Eriksson "Sven-Göran Eriksson") is sacked after one season as manager of Manchester City.

5 June 2008: [Mark Hughes](/wiki/Mark_Hughes "Mark Hughes") leaves Blackburn Rovers after four years to take over at Manchester City.

7 June 2008: [Plymouth Argyle](/wiki/Plymouth_Argyle_F.C. "Plymouth Argyle F.C.") goalkeeper [Luke McCormick](/wiki/Luke_McCormick_(footballer,_born_1983) "Luke McCormick (footballer, born 1983)"), 24, is involved in a car crash on the [M6 motorway](/wiki/M6_motorway "M6 motorway") in [Cheshire](/wiki/Cheshire "Cheshire") in which two children are killed and four other people are injured.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

22 June 2008: [Paul Ince](/wiki/Paul_Ince "Paul Ince") becomes manager of Blackburn Rovers, ending his season-long spell in charge of [Milton Keynes Dons](/wiki/Milton_Keynes_Dons_F.C. "Milton Keynes Dons F.C.") (where he won the [League Two](/wiki/Football_League_Two "Football League Two") title and the [Football League Trophy](/wiki/Football_League_Trophy "Football League Trophy").

## National team

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: National team")]

[England](/wiki/England_national_football_team "England national football team") played their first international match on a [synthetic pitch](/wiki/FieldTurf "FieldTurf") against [Russia](/wiki/Russia_national_football_team "Russia national football team") and lost the game 2–1,[[99]](#cite_note-99) leaving qualification for [UEFA Euro 2008](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_2008 "UEFA Euro 2008") out of their hands. On 21 November 2007, they lost 3–2 to [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia_national_football_team "Croatia national football team"), and consequently failed to qualify for Euro 2008 following [Russia](/wiki/Russia_national_football_team "Russia national football team")'s 1–0 win over [Andorra](/wiki/Andorra_national_football_team "Andorra national football team").[[100]](#cite_note-100) As a result, England manager Steve McClaren was sacked the following day.[[101]](#cite_note-101) [Fabio Capello](/wiki/Fabio_Capello "Fabio Capello") was confirmed as the new England manager on 14 December 2007. Capello was unveiled by the FA on 17 December 2007, and took up his new role on 7 January 2008.[[102]](#cite_note-102)

## League tables

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: League tables")]

### Premier League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Premier League")]

Main article: [2007–08 Premier League](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Premier_League "2007–08 Premier League")

[Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United_F.C. "Manchester United F.C.") were crowned league champions for the second year in succession, the tenth time in the history of the Premier League and the 17th time overall. They also won the [European Cup/UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") for the third time, and [Cristiano Ronaldo](/wiki/Cristiano_Ronaldo "Cristiano Ronaldo") finished as the league's top scorer with 31 goals. While all three of their main rivals kept the battle for the title close, [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_F.C. "Chelsea F.C.") had the more dramatic season; influential manager [José Mourinho](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Mourinho "José Mourinho") departed in mid-September and was replaced by [Avram Grant](/wiki/Avram_Grant "Avram Grant"), who became the first Chelsea manager in four years to go without a trophy. [Arsenal](/wiki/Arsenal_F.C. "Arsenal F.C."), meanwhile, after two seasons of disappointment, finished third, just missing out on the title by four points. Completing the top four was [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool_F.C. "Liverpool F.C."), which ensured that the same four teams qualified for Europe's elite competition once again.

[Everton](/wiki/Everton_F.C. "Everton F.C.") and [Aston Villa](/wiki/Aston_Villa_F.C. "Aston Villa F.C.") occupied the two qualification places for the UEFA Cup as managers [David Moyes](/wiki/David_Moyes "David Moyes") and [Martin O'Neill](/wiki/Martin_O%27Neill "Martin O'Neill") continued to impress for their respective sides (Villa qualified for the Intertoto Cup), whilst [Portsmouth](/wiki/Portsmouth_F.C. "Portsmouth F.C.") collected their first piece of silverware in 58 years by winning the FA Cup in their most successful season ever. [Tottenham](/wiki/Tottenham_Hotspur_F.C. "Tottenham Hotspur F.C.") shook off the shock sacking of [Martin Jol](/wiki/Martin_Jol "Martin Jol") with newly installed [Juande Ramos](/wiki/Juande_Ramos "Juande Ramos") winning them their first trophy in nine years in the League Cup and ensuring a third consecutive year of [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League") qualification, even if their league form was far from stellar.

[Manchester City](/wiki/Manchester_City_F.C. "Manchester City F.C.") went into the season with high expectations, with a new owner in [Thaksin Shinawatra](/wiki/Thaksin_Shinawatra "Thaksin Shinawatra") and a new manager in [Sven-Göran Eriksson](/wiki/Sven-G%C3%B6ran_Eriksson "Sven-Göran Eriksson"), alongside an influx of new talent, but finished 9th after suffering from inconsistent form at the turn of the year. Eriksson then lost his job at the end of the season, which included an 8–1 loss to [Middlesbrough](/wiki/Middlesbrough_F.C. "Middlesbrough F.C.") on the final day. [Newcastle](/wiki/Newcastle_United_F.C. "Newcastle United F.C.") welcomed the returning [Kevin Keegan](/wiki/Kevin_Keegan "Kevin Keegan") as manager after sacking [Sam Allardyce](/wiki/Sam_Allardyce "Sam Allardyce"), and while a winless run from Boxing Day left them four points off the relegation places in mid-March, the Magpies secured safety by winning four of their next seven games.

Newly promoted [Derby County](/wiki/Derby_County_F.C. "Derby County F.C."), twice champions of the old First Division, were relegated straight back to the Championship after just one season in the Premier League, winning just one game and collecting a mere eleven points all season; the team's season broke records for all the wrong reasons as they had the worst goal difference, the lowest number of goals scored in the top flight and the earliest post-war relegation. Their relegation was effectively confirmed when manager [Billy Davies](/wiki/Billy_Davies "Billy Davies") stood down in November. The departure of long-serving manager [Steve Bruce](/wiki/Steve_Bruce "Steve Bruce") and subsequent appointment of [Alex McLeish](/wiki/Alex_McLeish "Alex McLeish") meant that [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham_City_F.C. "Birmingham City F.C.") were relegated after a single season back in the top flight. Taking the final relegation spot were [Reading](/wiki/Reading_F.C. "Reading F.C."), who seemed safe until the last few weeks of the season, and only a year after narrowly missing out on a European spot. [Fulham](/wiki/Fulham_F.C. "Fulham F.C.") narrowly survived on goal difference, seemingly dead and buried until the final few games, the appointment of [Roy Hodgson](/wiki/Roy_Hodgson "Roy Hodgson"), who returned to English football after nine years managing in Europe, saw significant improvement enabling them to escape the drop.

### Football League Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Football League Championship")]

Main article: [2007–08 Football League Championship](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Football_League_Championship "2007–08 Football League Championship")

[West Bromwich Albion](/wiki/West_Bromwich_Albion_F.C. "West Bromwich Albion F.C.") won the Championship title and sealed promotion to the Premier League after a two-year absence. [Stoke City](/wiki/Stoke_City_F.C. "Stoke City F.C.") joined them, clinching promotion on the last day of the season and returning to the top flight of English football after an absence of 23 years. [Hull City](/wiki/Hull_City_A.F.C. "Hull City A.F.C.") reached the top flight for the first time in their history after beating [Bristol City](/wiki/Bristol_City_F.C. "Bristol City F.C.") 1–0 in the playoff final, marking their third promotion in five seasons having battled relegation last season. Bristol City had actually led the table at several points of the season but nonetheless their fourth-place finish proved a far cry from being the relegation favourites many had tipped them for.

[Wolverhampton Wanderers](/wiki/Wolverhampton_Wanderers_F.C. "Wolverhampton Wanderers F.C.") narrowly missed out on the play-offs on goal difference to a Watford side who actually led the league for most of the first half of the campaign but won just one of their last sixteen to sneak into the playoffs, with not even the arrival of top scorer [Sylvan Ebanks-Blake](/wiki/Sylvan_Ebanks-Blake "Sylvan Ebanks-Blake") helping Wolves' cause. [Ipswich Town](/wiki/Ipswich_Town_F.C. "Ipswich Town F.C.") lost just once at home but missed out despite a final day win over Hull. [Crystal Palace](/wiki/Crystal_Palace_F.C. "Crystal Palace F.C.") initially looked in danger of relegation until the appointment of [Neil Warnock](/wiki/Neil_Warnock "Neil Warnock") in October saw them climb the table and clinch a play-off spot at the expense of his old club [Sheffield United](/wiki/Sheffield_United_F.C. "Sheffield United F.C.").

[Colchester United](/wiki/Colchester_United_F.C. "Colchester United F.C.") could not build on last season's tenth-place finish and were relegated back to League One in bottom place after two years in this league. [Scunthorpe United](/wiki/Scunthorpe_United_F.C. "Scunthorpe United F.C.")'s return to the second tier was short lived as they made an immediate return to League One. [Leicester City](/wiki/Leicester_City_F.C. "Leicester City F.C.") went down on the final day despite drawing at Stoke, after Southampton beat Sheffield United to move above them. It meant that the Foxes would spend next season playing in the third tier of English football for the first time in their history, the employment of four permanent managers throughout the season saw them finally hit rock-bottom after several years of struggle and managerial changes. [Sheffield Wednesday](/wiki/Sheffield_Wednesday_F.C. "Sheffield Wednesday F.C."), [Coventry City](/wiki/Coventry_City_F.C. "Coventry City F.C.") and [Blackpool](/wiki/Blackpool_F.C. "Blackpool F.C.") also all survived on the last day, in Coventry's case despite losing 4–1 at [Charlton](/wiki/Charlton_Athletic_F.C. "Charlton Athletic F.C.").

### Football League One

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Football League One")]

Main article: [2007–08 Football League One](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Football_League_One "2007–08 Football League One")

Swansea City won the League One title after amassing 92 points, the highest by a Welsh club in the Football League. Nottingham Forest moved back up to the Championship after a three-season absence, winning six of their last seven and snatching promotion from Doncaster Rovers on the final day. Doncaster made amends for missing out on automatic promotion by winning the playoffs to enter the Championship after a half century absence from the second tier.

Leeds United's record would have seen them promoted at the expense of Forest, but they were cost dear by a 15-point deduction that was imposed pre-season after their failure to agree a deal with their creditors almost resulted in the club being ejected from the Football League entirely. The deduction led to many experts predicting they would suffer a second successive relegation, but those fears were quickly dismissed, as they wiped the deduction out by winning their first five games, and ultimately started the campaign with a 13-match unbeaten run. However, they lost to Doncaster in the playoff final, ending their hopes of an immediate return to the Championship. Carlisle proved to be the surprise package however and remained in contention for 2nd place until the final day, narrowly losing to Leeds in the play-offs.

Luton Town suffered their second consecutive relegation as they finished bottom and sat in administration for the entire season, resulting in a ten-point deduction. Port Vale, who were statistically the worst team in the division, joined them. Gillingham were relegated on the last day of the season, as were Bournemouth, the latter of whom also entered administration and suffered a ten-point deduction which proved fatal. Crewe narrowly escaped relegation courtesy of Bournemouth's points deduction and despite losing 4–1 on the final day. Cheltenham avoided the drop in part to their final day win that also cost Doncaster automatic promotion.

### Football League Two

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2007%E2%80%9308_in_English_football&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Football League Two")]

Main article: [2007–08 Football League Two](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_Football_League_Two "2007–08 Football League Two")

MK Dons won the League Two title, returning to the third tier after a two-season absence and earning their first major achievement in their four-year history. New boss Darren Ferguson led Peterborough United to promotion as runners-up. Hereford United also climbed out of the bottom division meaning they would be playing third-tier football for first time in thirty years next season. The final promotion place went to Stockport County, who won the playoffs.

At the bottom, Wrexham lost their 87-year League status. Mansfield Town had a good FA Cup run but could not cope in the league and were also relegated out of the league after 77 years.

Entering the Football League for the following season were Aldershot Town (the successor of the original [Aldershot](/wiki/Aldershot_F.C. "Aldershot F.C.") that folded during the 1991–92 season) and Exeter City, who returned after five years away.
